Our Story

The City Beside
the Perfect Cone

Legazpi City — officially spelled "Legazpi" — is the capital city of Albay province and the regional center of Bicol Region V. Dramatically positioned at the foot of the world-renowned Mayon Volcano, the city offers one of the most breathtaking urban backdrops in all of Asia.

Named after Spanish conquistador Miguel López de Legazpi, the city blends centuries of rich colonial heritage with the warm and resilient Bicolano spirit. From the smoldering cone of Mayon to the tranquil waters of Albay Gulf, Legazpi is a city where nature commands attention at every turn.

Province
Albay, Bicol
Region
Bicol Region (V)
Language
Bicolano · Filipino
Airport
Legazpi Airport (LGP)
2,463m Mayon's Summit
Must Experience

Legazpi's Finest Destinations

A volcano, ancient ruins, serene lakes, and a vibrant waterfront — Legazpi is nature and history perfectly fused.

Natural Wonder · Active Volcano
Mayon Volcano
The world's most perfectly-shaped stratovolcano — standing 2,463 meters above sea level with a nearly perfect symmetrical cone that has captivated visitors for centuries.
Heritage Ruins
Cagsawa Ruins
Haunting 18th-century church ruins buried by the 1814 Mayon eruption — one of the most photographed spots in the Philippines.
Scenic Lake
Sumlang Lake
A serene freshwater lake with stunning Mayon reflections — perfect for bamboo raft rides at golden hour.
Urban Waterfront
Embarcadero de Legazpi
A lively waterfront promenade with restaurants, ATV rides, and sweeping views of Albay Gulf and the volcano.
Adventure & Panorama
Lignon Hill Nature Park
Ziplines, nature trails, and the best 360° views of Mayon Volcano and Legazpi City — all in one protected hillside park.
Heritage & Festivals

Faith, Fire & Tradition

SEP
Grand Festival · Every September

Peñafrancia Festival

The grandest Marian festival in the Bicol Region — a nine-day celebration honoring Our Lady of Peñafrancia. Throughout Legazpi and all of Albay, the faithful gather for processions and novena Masses echoing the Bicolano cry: "Viva la Virgen!"

🕯️ One of Asia's Grandest Marian Festivals
JAN

Ibalong Festival

Albay's premier street festival celebrating the ancient Bicolano epic poem "Ibalong" — with indigenous costumes, tribal dances, and a grand parade honoring legendary heroes.

MAY

Magayon Festival

A month-long tourism festival named after the legendary Bicolana beauty Daragang Magayon — trade fairs, cultural shows, and food celebrations throughout Albay.

AUG

Legazpi City Charter Day

Annual commemoration of Legazpi's cityhood with civic programs, sports events, and a showcase of Bicolano arts and crafts in plazas across the city.

DEC

Bicolano Christmas Traditions

Simbang Gabi dawn masses, parol lantern processions, and caroling festivals make Christmas in Legazpi one of the most spirited in the entire Philippines.

Taste of Bicol
🌶️

Bicol Express — Born in the Bicol Region

Pork slow-cooked in rich coconut cream with generous chilies — fiery, fragrant, and addictive. Legazpi serves some of the most authentic versions of this iconic Bicolano dish that took the entire Philippines by storm.

🥥

Laing

Dried taro leaves simmered in coconut milk with chili and shrimp paste — the heart of Bicolano home cooking.

🍜

Kinalas

Legazpi's beloved noodle soup with braised pork in a dark, richly seasoned broth — a local breakfast staple.

🌿

Sinantolan

Shredded cotton fruit cooked in coconut milk with shrimp paste — a uniquely Bicolano delicacy.

🍡

Pili Nut Delicacies

Candied pili, pili tarts, pastillas — Legazpi's finest pasalubong, grown only in the rich volcanic soil of Bicol.

The Spiciest Table in the Philippines

Bicolano cuisine is legendary for its bold use of coconut milk and fire-hot chili peppers. In Legazpi, every dish is a product of the region's volcanic soil — lush, rich, and intensely flavored beyond compare.

The city's Sagpon Public Market and the Embarcadero food strip are the best places to taste authentic Bicolano cooking — from morning Kinalas vendors to smoky late-night Bicol Express eateries.

No visit is complete without savoring the pili nut — a buttery, earthy nut grown only in Bicol's fertile volcanic land, and the pride of Legazpi's confectionary tradition.

🌶️ Bicol Express Capital of the Philippines
Pride of Albay

What Legazpi City is Known For

🌋

Mayon Volcano

The world's most perfectly-shaped volcano — Legazpi's most iconic symbol and a UNESCO-recognized natural wonder that draws thousands of visitors annually.

🌶️

Bicol Express & Spicy Cuisine

Birthplace of the Philippines' most famous spicy dish. Legazpi's bold coconut-and-chili cooking style has influenced Filipino cuisine nationwide.

🥜

Pili Nut Capital

The pili nut — grown only in Bicol's rich volcanic soil — is Legazpi's prized export, used in candies, pastries, and delicacies loved across the Philippines.

⛵

Embarcadero Waterfront

A landmark lifestyle and tourism destination along Albay Gulf — famous for its ATV rides, dining strip, and panoramic views of Mayon Volcano over the water.

🏚️

Cagsawa Ruins

The hauntingly beautiful ruins of a 16th-century Franciscan church buried by the 1814 Mayon eruption — one of the most photographed heritage sites in the Philippines.

🎭

Ibalong & Magayon Festivals

Home to two of Albay's grandest festivals — the Ibalong Festival celebrating the ancient Bicolano epic, and Magayon Festival honoring Bicol's legendary beauty.

✈️

Gateway to Bicol Region

As Albay's capital city with its own international airport (LGP), Legazpi serves as the main entry point and regional hub for the entire Bicol Region.

🏄

Adventure Tourism

From Mayon Volcano trekking and ATV lava field rides to ziplines at Lignon Hill and bamboo rafting at Sumlang Lake — Legazpi is Bicol's adventure capital.

🛐

Marian Faith & Heritage

A deeply Catholic city known for the Peñafrancia Festival, grand Simbang Gabi traditions, and historic colonial-era churches scattered across the province.

Local Economy
🥜

Pili Nut Products

Candied pili, pili tarts, pastillas, pili brittle, and pili oil — Legazpi's most prized export and top pasalubong item, grown exclusively in Bicol's volcanic soil.

🥥

Coconut & VCO

Virgin coconut oil, coconut vinegar, and copra are major agricultural outputs from Legazpi's surrounding farms, widely traded throughout the Bicol region.

🌾

Rice & Root Crops

Palay (rice), gabi (taro), cassava, and sweet potato are staple crops cultivated in the fertile volcanic plains surrounding the city.

🐟

Marine & Aquatic Products

Fresh fish, shrimp, crab, and shellfish from Albay Gulf supply Legazpi's markets daily — the city's coastal location makes seafood a key part of its economy.

🧺

Abaca & Handicrafts

Abaca fiber weaving, bamboo products, rattan baskets, and traditional Bicolano crafts are produced by local artisans and sold at public markets.

🌶️

Processed Bicol Food Products

Packaged Bicol Express, laing, and sinantolan — bottled and exported as ready-to-eat delicacies from local food enterprises and cooperatives.

🪨

Volcanic Soil Agriculture

The mineral-rich volcanic soil of the Mayon Volcano area produces high-quality vegetables, fruits, and cacao unique to the Albay province.

A City Rich in Natural Produce

Legazpi City's economy is deeply rooted in its volcanic geography. The fertile plains surrounding Mayon Volcano yield some of the most nutrient-rich agricultural products in the Philippines, with the pili nut standing as the crown jewel of Bicol's produce.

Beyond agriculture, the city's coastal position along Albay Gulf sustains a thriving fishing industry. Local fishermen supply the Sagpon Public Market with the freshest catch daily — from prized bisugo (threadfin bream) to native crab and shrimp.

Small and medium enterprises (SMEs) in Legazpi have also grown significantly, producing packaged Bicolano food products, abaca handicrafts, and coconut-based goods that are distributed nationwide and exported abroad.

Come Experience
the Bicol Gateway

✈️

Getting Here

Legazpi Airport (LGP) has daily flights from Manila via Cebu Pacific, Philippine Airlines, and AirAsia. Flight time from Manila is approximately 1 hour.

📍

Location

Legazpi City, Albay Province
Bicol Region V
Philippines, 4500

🌋

Getting Around

Tricycles and jeepneys serve all city routes. Accredited Mayon Volcano tour guides and ATV rental operators are available at the Embarcadero and Cagsawa Ruins.

📞

Tourism Office

Legazpi City Tourism Office
City Hall, Rizal Street, Legazpi City
